semanticCore: Erasing, wiping out, effacing, canceling, or cleaning
quranicRelevance: Effacing night (Mahawna - 'famahawna ayata al-layli'), and God erasing falsehood ('wayamhu Allahu al-batila').

keyDerivations.meaning: To erase, efface, wipe out, cancel, nullify, or sweep away

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَمَحَوْنَا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنَا آيَةَ النَّهَارِ مُبْصِرَةً (18:12): Then We effaced/wiped out the sign of the night (dimmed moon) and made the sign of the day illuminating (sun).
discardedNoise: Erase marks on modern paper writing.
summaryEn: The root denotes erasing, applied to the cosmic dimming of the moon's light (Mahawna), and the erasure of historical falsehoods by divine truth.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الإزالة الكلية والأثر والمحو، واصفاً محو وطمس نور القمر الفيزيائي (فمحونا آية الليل)، ومحو الباطل بنور الحق الكلماتي.
rawText: مَحَاهُ 1 He effaced, erased, rased, obliterated, or cancelled, it; removed, or did away with, its impression or trace. (K.) === مَحَتِ الرِّيحُ عَنْهُ الأَسْقَامَ وَالدُّنُوبَ The wind made to pass away, or dispelled, the clouds. (TA.) === مَحَا الصُّبْحُ اللَّيْلَ Daybreak dispelled the night. (TA.) === مَحَاٱللّٰهُ عَنْهُ الأَسْقَامَ وَالدُّنُوبَ [God removed from him diseases and sins; as though He cancelled them]. (Msb in art. عفو.) === الإِحْسَانُ يَمْحُو الإِسَآءَةَ Beneficence effaces, obliterates, or cancels, evil conduct. (TA)
